_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 6-4 Pediatric Return of Spontaneous Circulation (Revised 06/06/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Pediatric Cardiac Protocol Section 6-4: Pediatric Return of Spontaneous Circulation — MDHHS Approved 6/6/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: This protocol should be followed for all pediatric cardiac arrests with ROSC. If arrest is of known traumatic origin, refer to Traumatic Arrest-Treatment Protocol and MCA Transport Protocol. After ROSC, patients should be stabilized on scene prior to transport — ideally at least 5 minutes before moving. Refer to Pediatric Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: Post-ROSC Pediatric Care
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Stabilize on scene ideally at least 5 minutes before moving — initiate ALS response if available
If ventilation assistance required: ventilate at 10–12 breaths per minute — do NOT hyperventilate
Monitor waveform ETCO2 — if ventilation assistance required, target ETCO2 35–45 mmHg per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol
Reassess patient continuously — if patient becomes pulseless: begin CPR and follow Pediatric Cardiac Arrest-Treatment Protocol
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
Start IV/IO NS or LR KVO
Treat hypotension with IV/IO fluid bolus 20 mL/kg consistent with Shock-Treatment Protocol
May perform 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection: BLS or Specialist — see 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol) — must NOT delay or take precedence over other critical assessments and interventions

If hypotension persists after IV/IO fluid bolus: administer push-dose epinephrine per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC not available: prepare 10 mcg/mL by adding 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL in 9 mL NS; administer 1 mcg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 10 mcg/mL solution); maximum dose 10 mcg (1 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate to age-appropriate SBP per MI-MEDIC; if MI-MEDIC unavailable: titrate SBP > 70 mmHg + (2 x age in years) up to 100 mmHg
Anticipate airway intolerance and prepare for patient sedation — if patient becomes agitated with advanced airway in place, refer to Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select provider level authorized to obtain 12-lead ECG post-ROSC. NOTE: 12-lead must not delay other critical pediatric interventions.

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO Fluid Bolus 20 mL/kg (Pediatric Post-ROSC)
key_drugs.contraindications: Signs of pulmonary edema or cardiogenic shock — reassess after bolus
key_drugs.notes: Consistent with Shock-Treatment Protocol. Reassess after each bolus. If hypotension persists, initiate push-dose epinephrine.

key_drugs.name: Push-Dose Epinephrine IV/IO (Pediatric Post-ROSC Hypotension)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 1 mcg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 10 mcg/mL solution) IV/IO; maximum dose 10 mcg (1 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate to age-appropriate SBP
key_drugs.indication: Hypotension persisting after IV/IO fluid bolus
key_drugs.contraindications: Use caution — ensure fluid bolus completed first
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for weight-based dosing. Preparation: add 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL (1:10,000) to 9 mL NS = 10 mcg/mL concentration. Maximum dose 10 mcg (1 mL). Age-appropriate SBP target: > 70 + (2 x age in years) up to 100 mmHg if MI-MEDIC unavailable.

deterioration_flags: Patient becomes pulseless — begin CPR immediately; follow Pediatric Cardiac Arrest-Treatment Protocol
Hypotension (SBP < 70 + 2 x age, or per MI-MEDIC) — IV/IO 20 mL/kg fluid bolus; if persists, push-dose epinephrine
ETCO2 < 35 mmHg — reduce ventilation rate; avoid hyperventilation
ETCO2 > 45 mmHg — increase ventilation rate; reassess airway
Agitation with advanced airway — Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL — IV dextrose per MI-MEDIC or Pediatric AMS weight/color table
scope_notes: Vital sign monitoring, SpO2, ETCO2, ventilation rate management: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
IV/IO fluid bolus 20 mL/kg: Specialist skill
Push-dose epinephrine: Specialist skill — use MI-MEDIC; max 10 mcg per dose
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist; must NOT delay other critical interventions
Advanced airway sedation: Specialist skill per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Age-appropriate SBP target (MI-MEDIC unavailable): > 70 + (2 x age in years) up to 100 mmHg
